channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: Improving LLMs via Validator-to-Generator Alignment
item.description: arXiv:2607.02668v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models are inconsistent: varying prompts or including unrelated information can lead to unexpected changes in model outputs. The generator-validator (G-V) gap is one manifestation of this phenomenon, where LLMs generate responses that t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, prompt, alignment, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Echoes of Unrest: A Multimodal NLP Framework for Early Warning of Fake News and Violence-Driven Mob Activity
item.description: arXiv:2607.02734v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Rapid growth in social media has transformed global communication by enabling fast information exchange, but it has also accelerated the spread of misinformation. Fake news, manipulated content, and provocative narratives are increasingly linked to so...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> multimodal, attention, arxiv, embedding, platform | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Reinforcement Learning for Data-Efficient Code-Switched ASR
item.description: arXiv:2607.02757v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Audio-language models can be prompted for code-switched speech, but their decoding is not optimized for code-switching and often fails at language boundaries. We propose a practical reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards recipe for data-effici...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, prompt, fine-tuning, audio | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Gemma 4 Technical Report
item.description: arXiv:2607.02770v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce Gemma 4, a new generation of open-weight, natively multimodal language models in the Gemma model family. Designed to advance compute efficiency and reasoning, the Gemma 4 model suite features dense and Mixture-of-Experts architectures, ra...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, multimodal, arxiv, memory, image | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Seduced by the Narrative: Assessing Rule Adherence in Semi-Open Textual Sandboxes
item.description: arXiv:2607.02802v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As LLMs are increasingly deployed as autonomous adjudicators in semi-open textual game environments, robust rule adherence becomes critical when user intent conflicts with system rules. However, these models are trained to be helpful and compliant, le...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> vector, model, GPT, LLM, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Jointly Improving Dialect Identification and ASR in Indian Languages using Multimodal Feature Fusion
item.description: arXiv:2607.02862v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) and Dialect Identification (DID) are crucial for Indian languages, many of which are low-resource and exhibit significant dialectal differences. Existing methods often optimize ASR or DID individually, resulting in p...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, multimodal, attention, arxiv, embedding | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PraMem: Practice-derived Experiential Memory for Long-horizon Behavior Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2607.02881v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-horizon behavior prediction aims to infer a user's next action based on a lengthy historical sequence, playing a crucial role in artificial intelligence field. The rise of large language models (LLMs) offers a promising direction for sequential b...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, compression, memory, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Where do LLMs Fall Short in CBT-Guided Affective Reasoning?
item.description: arXiv:2607.02885v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) provides a structured framework for understanding a user's mental state by examining the interaction between cognitive and behavioral factors. However, out-of-the-box LLMs respond fluently and empathetically, yet col...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, reasoning, prompt, prompting | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Distill Where the Student Goes: Teacher-Regularized RL for English-Evidence Cross-Lingual RAG
item.description: arXiv:2607.02966v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Cross-lingual retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) is often deployed in an English-evidence regime, where users query in diverse languages but retrieved passages remain English. In this setting, generation can fail despite strong base models: English ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, vision, RAG, augmented | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: iFLYTEK-Embodied-Omni Technical Report
item.description: arXiv:2607.02542v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: General-purpose embodied agents must understand multimodal instructions, anticipate how their environment will evolve, and produce precise control actions over extended horizons. Existing approaches typically specialize in visual-language reasoning, v...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, demonstration, attention, multimodal, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ASK in the Dark: Uncertainty-Gated LLM Assistance under Partial Observability
item.description: arXiv:2607.02686v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement learning agents operating under partial observability must act on incomplete information, making them natural candidates for guidance from small language models (SLMs) that carry broad reasoning priors. Yet integrating SLM guidance into ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, LLM, arxiv, prompt, chain-of-thought | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SwarmResearch: Orchestrating Coding Agents for Open-Ended Discovery
item.description: arXiv:2607.02807v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-running coding agents such as autoresearch can persistently discover optimizations for open-ended problems. However, they tend to converge onto a single high-level approach, then proceed with low-level edits while missing other superior approache...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> research, arxiv, LLM, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Object-Centric Environment Modeling for Agentic Tasks
item.description: arXiv:2607.02846v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents can improve through accumulated experience, but free-form textual memories become difficult to maintain, validate, and reuse as interactions grow. Recent symbolic approaches learn executable skills or programmatic wor...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, large language model, RAG, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MedCalc-Pro: Solving Complex Medical Calculations with LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2607.02879v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Current benchmarks for evaluating large language models (LLMs) in medical calculation are largely based on simplified settings, where each patient case corresponds to a single calculator and the required tool is explicitly specified in the query. Howe...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, tool, large language model, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Oyster-II: Reinforcement Learning for Constructive Safety Alignment in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.02914v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities across diverse applications, yet ensuring their simultaneous safety, helpfulness, and trustworthiness remains a persistent challenge. Conventional refusal-oriented alignment strate...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, reasoning, alignment, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Auditing the Audit: Five Failure Modes in Benchmark-Validity Audits
item.description: arXiv:2607.02586v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Governance frameworks ask AI providers and auditors for documented evaluation evidence, and perturbation-based construct-validity audits are a common form of that evidence. We argue the audits are themselves fragile: their conclusions can be silently ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, study, framework, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Evaluating Time Series Foundation Models for Electricity Price Forecasting: Contamination Risk, Distributional Shifts, and Covariate Dependence
item.description: arXiv:2607.02623v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Time series foundation models (TSFMs) have shown strong zero-shot forecasting performance, but their generalization in covariate-driven, non-stationary settings is underexplored. Electricity price forecasting (EPF) presents a challenging testbed due t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, zero-shot, arxiv, framework, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: QuantFlow: A Federated Mamba-Based Post-Transformer Foundation Model for Time-Series Forecasting
item.description: arXiv:2607.02632v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Time-series forecasting supports decisions in finance, en-ergy, transportation, public health, and industrial monitoring. Recent foundation models improve transfer across forecast-ing tasks, but many depend on centralized data and Trans-former attenti...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, attention, arxiv, embedding, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Post-Generation Curation of Synthetic Images via Homogeneous-Heterogeneous Splitting
item.description: arXiv:2607.02637v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent generative models can produce high-quality synthetic images, offering scalable training training data for data-hungry models. Existing approaches to exploiting this potential typically involve 1) training or fine-tuning generators, or 2) using ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, prompt, alignment, image | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context window, prompt, context, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, augmented, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> vector, model, framework, RAG, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.

item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: LuxSQA: Ask Me in Luxembourgish with TTS-Augmented Spoken Question Answering
item.description: arXiv:2607.02763v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Spoken Question Answering (SQA) remains largely focused on high-resource languages and carefully recorded speech, limiting the reach of speech-LLM methods in low-resource settings. This paper investigates whether text-to-speech (TTS) can provide task-...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, paper, audio, augmented, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: VERITAS: Towards a General-Purpose Replication Tool for Scientific Research
item.description: arXiv:2607.02931v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI tools are accelerating scientific publication while the systems that review it struggle to keep up, and independent verification of published research has become both harder and more important. As manual replication is slow and expensive, a growing...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, research, arxiv, tool, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: GRAFT: Grafted Reference Audio for Fine-grained Pronunciation in Zero-shot Text-to-Speech
item.description: arXiv:2607.02633v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present GRAFT, a per-word pronunciation conditioning mechanism for text-to-speech neural codec language modeling. Existing systems reach high intelligibility and naturalness but inherit the ambiguity of text and mispronounce rare proper nouns, loan...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, prompt, study, audio | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Federated Learning for Object Detection: Enabling Collaborative Drone Learning Without Centralizing Data
item.description: arXiv:2607.02636v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Object detection is a fundamental capability for AI-driven perception in safety-critical drone and edge-vision systems, including disaster response, operational security environments, infrastructure monitoring and defense applications. Robust model pe...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, platform, image, vision | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Weighted Conformal Prediction for Lab-to-Track Thermal Transfer in EV Motorsport Powertrains
item.description: arXiv:2607.02722v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Predicting thermal volatility in high-performance EV powertrains is difficult as internal temperatures are rarely observable outside the lab, and models calibrated on lab drive cycles fail when deployed against real-world loads. We study this lab-to-t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, tool, study, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Out-of-Distribution Generalization of Risk Aversion in Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.02755v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Training AIs to be risk-averse in resources could offer a failsafe in the event that AIs turn out misaligned. Misaligned but risk-averse AIs would tend to prefer low-risk, low-reward strategies like cooperation over high-risk, high-reward strategies l...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, alignment, experiment, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.

item.title: Internal Pluralism and the Limits of Pairwise Comparisons
item.description: arXiv:2607.02672v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Local pairwise comparisons are a standard tool for learning how people want decision rules to work, e.g., in participatory design or alignment. However, their use builds in two strong assumptions: that local comparisons are sufficient evidence about h...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, alignment, tool, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Automated Data Readiness for Scientific AI
item.description: arXiv:2607.02771v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Leadership computing facilities steward large-scale scientific datasets that routinely require substantial transformation before serving as AI training data. However, no existing framework fully unifies automated transformation, readiness assessment, ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> platform, tool, arxiv, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Sliding-Window-Based Reinforcement Learning for Dynamic Assembly Flow Shop Scheduling with Multi-Product Delivery
item.description: arXiv:2607.02941v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-product kitting delivery imposes significant challenges for real-time scheduling in hybrid manufacturing systems that integrate processing and assembly, as dynamic order arrivals simultaneously alter supply dependencies and the set of feasible j...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, product, paper, framework, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>

item.title: A Granularity-Aware EEG Feature Framework for Psychopathology Dimension Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2607.02670v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Electroencephalography (EEG) offers a noninvasive approach for examining neurophysiological correlates of dimensional psychopathology, yet systematic evidence across EEG paradigms and feature granularities remains limited. Here, we develop a granulari...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, attention, arxiv, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>

item.title: code-review-graph - Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI. Builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, with benchmarked context reductions on reviews and large-repo workflows.

item.title: WrenAI - GenBI (Generative BI) for AI agents, an open-source, governed text-to-SQL through an open context layer that turns natural-language questions into trusted dashboards, charts, and SQL across 20+ data sources, such as BigQuery, Snowflake, PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, Amazon Redshift, Databricks and more.
item.description: GenBI (Generative BI) for AI agents, an open-source, governed text-to-SQL through an open context layer that turns natural-language questions into trusted dashboards, charts, and SQL across 20+ data sources, such as BigQuery, Snowflake, PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, Amazon Redshift, Databricks and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)
